etymology.description: phonetic 彥 (yàn) + semantic 頁 ("head") -> face
etymology.interpretation: The character 顏(yán, "face") combines 彥(yàn, "a handsome man") as the phonetic element with 頁(yè, "head") as the semantic component. The concept of a 'head' relates directly to the idea of a 'face', suggesting the appearance or visage of a person.
etymology.simplification: 顔(顏) was simplified by replacing 彥(顏) with 彦(彦) and 頁(顏) with 页(页) for fewer strokes.

examples.chinese: 他的脸色看起来很好(他的臉色看起來很好)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de liǎnsè kàn qǐlái hěn hǎo.

examples.chinese: 她害羞时(她害羞時)，整个脸都红了(整個臉都紅了)。
examples.pinyin: Tā hàixiū shí，zhěnggè liǎn dōu hóng le.
examples.english: When she's shy, her whole face turns red.

examples.chinese: 这个面霜能够改善肤色(這個面霜能夠改善膚色)。
examples.english: This face cream can improve skin tone.
